hook.text: Serializable ensures transactions behave as if they ran one after another. No concurrency anomalies at all.

buildup.text: The database detects conflicts between concurrent transactions and aborts one of them to maintain correctness.

discovery.text: Postgres uses Serializable Snapshot Isolation (SSI). It detects dependency cycles and rolls back the victim.

twist.text: Serializable transactions can fail due to serialization errors. Your app must retry them.

climax.text: Use serializable for critical invariants: account balances, inventory counts, seat reservations.

punchline.text: Serializable: maximum safety, but your app must handle retries.

quiz.question: What must your application do when using serializable isolation?
options: Handle serialization errors by retrying the failed transaction
Open a new database connection for each query
